talk_title: Biospecimen science funding opportunities and Moonshot Biobank overview

speaker.name: Lokesh Agrawal
speaker.affiliation: Acting Chief, Biorepositories and Biospecimen Research Branch, NCI

models.name: Cancer Moonshot Biobank
models.genotype_or_key_features: pan‑cancer cohort of ~1,300 patients with longitudinal blood and tissue samples; includes solid and hematologic malignancies

clinical_data.population: U01 mechanism grants across cancer types

clinical_data.n: n=1,020 subjects with longitudinal blood or tissue

MOA_summary: Dr. Agrawal described the NCI biospecimen science infrastructure that standardizes pre‑analytical processes, provides curated specimen banks (NCCN, CHTN, Moonshot), and funds multidisciplinary U01 projects to generate reproducible, clinically annotated biospecimens for assay validation across solid and hematologic cancers.
key_findings: The U01 biospecimen science program has funded 24 grants covering a broad range of cancer types.
From 2022‑2024, the CHTN network served 618 unique researchers, distributing 117 specimens and supporting 122 publications.
The Cancer Moonshot Biobank enrolled ~1,300 patients, with 1,020 having longitudinal biospecimens, including ~300 with multiple blood draws and ~150 with paired tissue collections.
Diversity metrics of the Moonshot cohort show 18% Black/African American and 8% Hispanic participants, exceeding typical US trial representation.
